title: heleniastrum mexicanum
intro: Heleniastrum mexicanum, also known as Mexican prickly poppy or Mexican poppy, is a flowering plant native to Mexico. It belongs to the family Papaveraceae and is used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant effects. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used in herbal preparations to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems and skin conditions. The herbal preparations of Heleniastrum mexicanum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to relieve symptoms and promote overall well-being.
